items.title: ‘This is dangerous’: Anwar warns Malaysians against mistaking social media snippets for knowledge
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 29 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today warned that children are losing the habit of reading as social media shortens attention spans and replaces knowledge with bite-sized information. Launching the 2026 National Book Fair at the World Trade Centre here, Anwar who professed himself a bookworm, urged schools, teachers and parents to reignite interest in reading to build a more informed society. “The reading culture has become a problem in the modern world,” he said.

items.title: Govt can only advise, not force companies to adopt WFH, says HR minister
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Human Resources Minister Datuk Seri Ramanan Ramakrishnan said the government cannot compel private companies to adopt work-from-home (WFH) policies as businesses are best placed to determine their productivity needs. He said Putrajaya could only encourage and advise employers to adopt flexible working arrangements amid concerns over the global economic climate. Ramanan said the government would continue encouraging companies to adopt WFH arrangements following repeated reminders by Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim.

items.title: "Please help mummy and daddy," woman pleads after court sets RM240,000 bail
items.summary: BUTTERWORTH: A woman broke down in tears inside the Sessions Court here today, pleading with her children to find help to raise the RM240,000 bail set for her and her husband after both were charged with trafficking Indonesian migrant workers for forced labour. "Please help mummy and daddy. Chan also personally claimed trial to four similar charges in the same court.

items.title: Storm warning out for Kedah, Terengganu, Kelantan, Johor, Sarawak, Sabah
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 29 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a thunderstorm warning for seven states today, with heavy rain and strong winds expected to persist until 7.00 pm. Central and southern states are also affected, specifically Jelebu, Kuala Pilah, Jempol, and Tampin in Negeri Sembilan, alongside Tangkak, Segamat, Muar, Batu Pahat, Kluang, Mersing, and Kota Tinggi in Johor. In East Malaysia, MetMalaysia has flagged several divisions in Sarawak, namely Sibu, Mukah (Matu, Dalat, and Mukah), Kapit (Song and Kapit), Bintulu (Tatau), and Miri (Subis, Beluru, Miri, and Marudi).

items.title: Najib seeks to adduce new evidence in RM2.27b 1MDB appeal
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Former prime minister Datuk Seri Najib Razak has moved to introduce fresh evidence in his appeal against the conviction and sentence imposed on him in the 1Malaysia Development Bhd (1MDB) case involving RM2.27 billion. The new evidence centres on the involvement of businessman Low Taek Jho, better known as Jho Low, and former 1MDB general counsel Jasmine Loo Ai Swan. The application was filed at the Court of Appeal by Najib's legal team from Shafee & Co.

items.title: Apex Equity to raise RM14.07mil via private placement for money-lending business
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Apex Equity Holdings Bhd has proposed to undertake a private placement of up to 10 per cent or up to 21.36 million new Apex shares to raise up to RM14.07 million. The placement proceeds to be raised from the proposed private placement are expected to be utilised by Apex and its subsidiaries for the money lending business (RM13.52 million) and estimated expenses for the proposed private placement (RM558,000). It said the proposed private placement will serve as an additional source of funding for the group without incurring interest expenses as compared to conventional means of debt financing, in addition to strengthening the capital structure of the group by increasing the capital base of the group.

items.title: Mitra allocates RM66mil for six Indian community socioeconomic programmes
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Indian Community Transformation Unit (Mitra) has allocated RM65.5 million to roll out six key socioeconomic development initiatives spanning health, education, and entrepreneurship. Human Resources Minister Datuk Seri R. Ramanan said the programmes, which are expected to benefit at least 50,000 recipients, were made possible following Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim's announcement of an additional RM50 million allocation on May 10. He said Mitra previously received only RM5 million for such support.

items.title: Stepfather faces five charges of rape, sexual assault against teen stepdaughter
items.summary: MUAR: A teenage girl's decision to lodge a police report led to her stepfather being charged in the magistrate's court here today with five counts of rape and physical sexual assault against her since 2022. The 47-year-old plantation worker pleaded guilty to four counts of physical sexual assault before magistrate Arthana Sarma Rao, but claimed trial to the rape charge. On the first, second, third, and fifth charges, the accused was charged under Section 14 (a), (b), and (d) of the Sexual Offences Against Children Act 2017 for committing physical sexual assault against the teenager, who is now 15.

items.title: Missing witness sinks case against two men accused of possessing 150kg of drugs
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, May 29 — Two former steamboat restaurant workers were acquitted and freed by the High Court here today after a judge ruled the prosecution failed to secure a key witness sought by the defence in a case involving nearly 150kg of methamphetamine and heroin. Chan Shi Wei, 29, and Lim Chan Hau, 30, had spent six years in remand over the charges linked to a house in Kota Damansara. Bhupindar also found the prosecution failed to effectively challenge key aspects of the defence during cross-examination.

items.title: Anwar: Sabah's 40 per cent rights to be clarified tomorrow
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said Sabah's 40 per cent rights entitlement is expected to be clarified tomorrow. "The 40 per cent Sabah rights issue will be explained tomorrow in Sabah," he said after performing prayers at the Saidina Hamzah Mosque in Batu today. He was responding to a question on the Special Grant rate for Sabah.

items.title: Wong Chen questions sudden, 'uncourteous' removal as MDV chairman
items.summary: SUBANG JAYA: Subang member of parliament Wong Chen questioned his sudden removal as non-executive chairman of Malaysia Debt Ventures Bhd, following what he described as improvements in the company's performance. He said the previous chairman appointed by Khairy Jamaluddin was allowed to complete his term even after the change of government. Wong, who was appointed MDV non-executive chairman effective May 23, 2023, said the government-linked financial institution had been struggling financially when he first joined.

items.title: Loke calls for tougher laws against cable theft
items.summary: "The Home Ministry must strengthen existing laws to deter those who threaten the safety of the country's public transport network," he said at a press conference at the Chan Sow Lin LRT station today. He said the Transport Ministry had already raised the matter with the Home Ministry and would continue to push for firmer action. He said the matter remained under the Home Ministry's purview, but the Transport Ministry would continue to engage with the ministry on further action.

items.title: Man charged with trafficking four people, including three minors, for sex exploitation
items.summary: SANDAKAN: A 32-year-old man was charged in the magistrate's court here today with trafficking four people, including three underage girls, for sexual exploitation. Chu faces three charges of trafficking minors for sexual exploitation, involving two 15-year-olds, one a Malaysian and the other a foreigner, and a 17-year-old foreigner. For the fourth charge, he was accused of trafficking a 30-year-old foreign woman for sexual exploitation under Section 12 of Act 670, which provides for a jail term of up to 20 years and a fine upon conviction.

items.title: 'Just you wait': Man fined RM1,500 for threatening Perkeso officer
items.summary: BATU PAHAT: A car wash owner was fined RM1,500 by the magistrate's court here today after pleading guilty to obstructing a Social Security Organisation (Perkeso) officer from discharging his duties last week. Azim was accused of raising his voice and accused the 32-year-old male Perkeso inspector of conducting an inspection before telling him, "I remember your face, you wait". The accused paid the fine.

items.title: Perlis MB: I shot rampaging cow to protect the public
items.summary: KANGAR: Perlis Menteri Besar Abu Bakar Hamzah has clarified that he was forced to shoot a sacrificial cow to prevent the animal from injuring volunteers and members of the public. Abu Bakar explained the incident, which occurred during the Kuala Perlis State Constituency Perdana Korban Programme, to clear up public misunderstandings following a viral video of the event. "We organised a sacrificial programme involving 25 cows.

items.title: Review rail governance to restore public trust after LRT disruption, says advocate
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The recent LRT train service disruption should prompt a broader review of rail reliability governance, said MY Mobility Vision founder and senior adviser Wan Agyl Wan Hassan. He said Malaysia should move beyond a project-centric transport mindset. Wan Agyl said the country must place "equal emphasis on operational resilience, maintenance discipline, and asset lifecycle sustainability" to ensure the long-term reliability of its rail network.

items.title: RM24m Bukit Jalil stadium upgrade project under MACC scrutiny after complaints
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 29 — The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) today confirmed it is reviewing complaints linked to the evaluation process and upgrade works at the National Stadium Bukit Jalil. It said it is examining related information and documents before deciding on further investigation, Berita Harian reported this afternoon. “MACC always takes seriously any allegations involving corruption, abuse of power and misconduct affecting public interest and government procurement,” it was quoted as saying.

items.title: Son of Taiping Prison inmate Gan Chin Eng says ‘no closure’ despite Suhakam inquiry findings
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 29 — The son of inmate Gan Chin Eng, who died in Taiping Prison in January last year, says he has yet to find closure after the Human Rights Commission of Malaysia (Suhakam) released its findings on the case. Suhakam’s public inquiry reported evidence of abuse and negligence, pointing to systemic shortcomings in the prison’s management and administration. Gan, 62, died following the incident.

items.title: Loke pledges to take full responsibility for Chan Sow Lin LRT derailment [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Transport Minister Anthony Loke has pledged to take full responsibility for the Chan Sow Lin LRT derailment. "I will not shy away from accountability even though the incident was deeply disappointing," he said at a press conference at the Chan Sow Lin LRT station today. Responsibility means ensuring the right actions are taken," he said.

items.title: Video of Perlis MB shooting sacrificial cow sparks police probe
items.summary: KANGAR, May 29 — Perlis Menteri Besar Abu Bakar Hamzah is set to be questioned by police after a video clip showed him shooting a cow with a rifle during an Aidiladha sacrifice ceremony yesterday. Abu Bakar admitted to shooting the cow but said did it to prevent anyone from getting hurt after the animal had broken loose. Kangar police chief Assistant Commissioner Yusharifuddin Mohd Yusop said investigations have started, Berita Harian reported this afternoon.

items.title: OCK posts RM11.3mil Q3 profit on RM174.7mil revenue
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: OCK Group Bhd posted a net profit of RM11.25 million on revenue of RM174.7 million for the third quarter ended March 31, 2026 (Q3 FY26). For the nine-month period, the telecommunications network provider recorded a net profit of RM27.36 million on revenue of RM499.9 million. OCK group managing director Datuk Sam Ooi Chin Khoon said the earnings reflect the company's strengthening position within the telecommunications and digital infrastructure ecosystem.

items.title: Decomposing body found wrapped in mattress in shophouse
items.summary: MELAKA: A potential tenant looking to rent a shophouse in Kampung Padang, Pokok Mangga here, discovered a decomposing body wrapped in a mattress yesterday. Melaka police chief Datuk Dzulkhairi Mukhtar said police received a report at about 7pm from the premises owner, who had gone to inspect the property with the person. A police team dispatched to the scene discovered the body wrapped inside a folded mattress.

items.title: Bull turns on former owner in Kluang attack
items.summary: KLUANG: A 65-year-old man was injured after being gored by a raging bull at Kampung Murni Jaya, Layang-Layang, near here, on Tuesday. The man had earlier sold the bull to another villager. In another incident, a 45-year-old man in Kampung Sungai Tiram, Ulu Tiram, was injured after an aggressive buffalo attacked him.

items.title: Bakery products maker HSS Holdings to raise RM13.5mil from IPO
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Bakery products manufacturer HSS Holdings Bhd, en route to a listing on Bursa Malaysia's ACE Market, aims to raise RM13.5 million through its initial public offering (IPO). The company said the IPO comprises a public issue of 75 million new shares and an offer for sale of 52.5 million existing shares, priced at 18 sen per share. Of the total proceeds, RM2.57 million will be utilised for capital expenditure for the group's manufacturing facilities; RM4.50 million for the repayment of bank borrowings; RM2.93 million for working capital; and RM3.50 million for the defrayment of listing expenses, it said in a statement.

items.title: Laos transitions from backpackers haven to global coffee producer
items.summary: Diligent farmers are transforming the Lao coffee industry into a multimillion-dollar sector that mostly grows Robusta coffee and some Arabica varieties in the southern Bolaven Plateau. Last weekend, along with France and the European Union (EU), Laos launched the 2026 International Tea and Coffee Day themed "Two Brews, One Culture" in Vientiane. Laos coffee exports reach about 20 international markets.

items.title: IJN's Dr Jeswant becomes first Malaysian elected to prestigious global thoracic surgery body
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: For more than three decades, Professor Datuk Seri Dr Jeffrey Jeswant Dillon has spent countless hours in operating theatres handling some of Malaysia's most complex cardiothoracic surgery cases, while balancing the demands of clinical practice, academia, research and mentorship. Earlier this month, the senior consultant cardiothoracic surgeon at Institut Jantung Negara (IJN) added another milestone to his distinguished career when he became the first Malaysian elected as a member of the prestigious American Association for Thoracic Surgery (AATS), placing Malaysia on the global cardiothoracic surgery map. For Dr Jeswant, the recognition represents more than a professional accolade, reflecting his unwavering commitment and dedication to advancing cardiothoracic surgery both in Malaysia and internationally.

items.title: Form Four boy held for supplying drug-laced vape to girlfriend
items.summary: MELAKA: Police have detained a Form Four schoolboy suspected of supplying drug-laced vapes to his 14-year-old girlfriend. Melaka police chief Datuk Dzulkhairi Mukhtar said the 17-year-old was arrested yesterday and has been remanded for five days to assist in investigations. He said police are still conducting further investigations, including tracking down the source of the vape supply believed to have been obtained by the suspect.

items.title: Journalists on frontline in defending information integrity – Fahmi
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said journalists must remain on the front line in defending the integrity of information at a time when slander can spread instantly, and perception often outweighs reality. "The role of journalists is not confined to recording events, but also acts as an important voice in upholding the truth when society is confronted with confusion and speculation," he said in a Facebook post in conjunction with National Journalists' Day (Hawana) today. It said ethical journalism plays an important role in strengthening national unity, harmony and stability.

items.title: Cops probe viral video of Perlis MB shooting sacrificial cow with shotgun
items.summary: KANGAR: Police have confirmed they have yet to receive a report regarding a viral video allegedly showing Perlis Menteri Besar Abu Bakar Hamzah shooting a sacrificial cow with a shotgun, though a preliminary investigation into the incident is underway. District police chief Assistant Commissioner Yusharifuddin Mohd Yusop said police are aware of the video recording, which has been circulating on social media since yesterday. "The incident is already within the knowledge of the police.

items.title: MACC examining documents tied to Bukit Jalil National Stadium upgrade
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Upgrading works at the National Stadium Bukit Jalil are under scrutiny following a Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) probe into the procurement process. The MACC confirmed it received a report regarding the evaluation and implementation process of the upgrading works, as reported by a local news portal yesterday. Yesterday, Free Malaysia Today reported that the RM24 million upgrade project at the stadium had come under scrutiny after a contractor lodged a report with the MACC.

items.title: Ahead of official birthday, Friday sermon highlights King’s role as active guardian of stability and sovereignty
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 29 — The Yang di-Pertuan Agong not only symbolises the sovereignty of the nation, but is also a guardian who protects the citizens, safeguards the religion and is the mainstay of the nation's stability. “Indeed, religious and royal institutions form the key foundations that preserve the nation's sovereignty, the people's unity and the sanctity of the religion of Islam in this blessed nation. If leaders protect the nation, citizens should preserve the nation's soul,” the sermon read.

items.title: OSK Q1 net profit rises 11pct to RM138.04mil
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: OSK Holdings Bhd posted a higher net profit in the first quarter ended March 31, 2026 (1Q FY2026) by 11 pct to RM138.04 million, supported by a stronger operating profit from the property and financial services businesses, and higher investment income from the group's stake in RHB Bank Bhd. OSK has diversified business interests in property, financial services, industries, hospitality, and investment holding. During the quarter, operating profit grew 16 per cent to RM80.8 million, while profit from associates rose 10 per cent to RM92.4 million.

items.title: Malaysia's reserve assets edge higher to US$129.7bil in April
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia's official reserve assets rose to US$129.73 billion at the end of April 2026 from US$126.61 billion a month earlier, while other foreign currency assets stood at US$66.6 million, Bank Negara Malaysia (Bank Negara) said. The central bank said pre-determined short-term outflows of foreign currency loans, securities and deposits over the next 12 months totalled US$8.22 billion. It said these projected foreign currency inflows amount to US$2.98 billion in the next 12 months.

items.title: Top China leaders skip Asia defence summit headlined by US
items.summary: SINGAPORE: Pentagon chief Pete Hegseth is the headline speaker at Asia's premier defence summit opening Friday, but China's top officials aren't expected despite weighty questions like Taiwan and the war in Iran. Beijing's defence minister is to skip the three-day Shangri-La Dialogue in Singapore for the second year running, which analysts viewed as a sign of China's rising power. Defence Minister Dong Jun's absence means no meeting there with Hegseth as China warns the US over its involvement with Taiwan and Washington seeks an end to the Mideast war.

items.title: Selangor govt urged to explain below-market land sale near forest reserve
items.summary: Subang member of parliament Wong Chen said documents obtained through Memorandum of Transfer (MoT) searches revealed that the land was transacted at RM13.80 per sq ft. However, he said the Inland Revenue Board (LHDN), in calculating the ad valorem duty on May 5, 2019, had valued the land at RM55 per sq ft. "If we use the LHDN valuation of RM55 per sq ft, the Selangor government should have sold the land to Perano for around RM404 million instead of RM101 million. "Thus, the Selangor government had in fact sold the land at a heavily discounted value to Perano in 2012 and, despite the court ruling in 2017, then proceeded to complete the deal and transfer the land to Perano in 2019 and 2020," he said at a press conference here today.

items.title: Kazakhstan's Air Astana boosts Asia network as Middle East crisis reshapes transit flows
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Air Astana is accelerating its expansion across key Southeast Asia markets while positioning Kazakhstan as a growing transit hub between Europe and Asia amid shifting traffic flows due to geopolitical tensions in the Middle East. Air Astana Group chief executive officer Ibrahim Canliel said the airline had swiftly redeployed capacity towards stronger-performing markets including China, Southeast Asia and South Korea. Canliel said the airline group is continuing to evaluate additional destinations in the region including Kuala Lumpur as part of its broader expansion strategy.

items.title: RHB Bank Q1 profit rises 14.2pct on stronger income, credit quality
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: RHB Bank Bhd recorded a 14.2 per cent increase in net profit for the quarter ended March 31, 2026 (Q1 FY26), driven by steady income growth, disciplined cost management and improved credit quality. The bank's total income amounted to RM2.2 billion, supported by contributions from both fund-based and non-fund-based income streams. Its net fund-based income rose 4.6 per cent year-on-year (YoY) to RM1.6 billion, driven by 6.2 per cent YoY growth in gross loans and lower funding costs, while net interest margin, supported by liability management, stood at 1.91 per cent.

items.title: Anwar says budget cuts to Education Ministry unavoidable but manageable
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 29 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said today he saw no reason why the Ministry of Education cannot manage the impending cuts of its RM20 billion annual budget, as his government is forced to slash spending to cope with the economic fallout from war in the Middle East. “You can appeal as much as you want but the cuts will still happen,” Anwar told top officials of the ministry who attended the 2026 National Book Fair here. The budget cuts drew some criticism, but Anwar said the cuts will only affect “non-critical” spending.

items.title: NRD corrects ‘Islam’ status on Christian woman’s MyPR card — with the exact same mistake
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 29 — An Indonesian Christian woman today came to the National Registration Department (NRD) headquarters in Putrajaya with the hope of receiving a new permanent resident identification card (MyPR) that would correctly reflect her religious status as a non-Muslim. The 68-year-old woman, who prefers to be known as Yanti* and is the widow of a Malaysian man, was previously given a MyPR card with the word “Islam” printed. But Yanti was left disappointed after coming all the way from Johor by car today, as the NRD issued her a new MyPR card with the exact same error as her old MyPR card.

items.title: Najib files for new evidence in bid to overturn 1MDB conviction
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 29 — Former prime minister Datuk Seri Najib Razak has filed an application to introduce fresh evidence before the Court of Appeal for his bid to overturn his conviction on all 25 charges of abuse of power and money laundering linked to the 1MDB scandal. In a legal context, “fresh evidence” refers to information discovered after a trial concludes. Najib is seeking to introduce evidence concerning fugitive financier Low Taek Jho, better known as Jho Low, and former 1MDB legal counsel Jasmine Loo into his appeal hearing.

items.title: Finance Ministry warns of jail time, heavy fine over BUDI95 MyKad identity theft
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 29 — The Ministry of Finance has warned of a RM20,000 fine and three years’ jail for anyone caught misusing another person’s MyKad to claim BUDI MADANI RON95 (BUDI95) fuel subsidies, following a spike in identity theft reports at petrol stations. The MoF said it has also directed all oil retail companies to immediately tighten compliance with standard operating procedures (SOPs) for MyKad verification. “Any transaction carried out manually without prior physical MyKad verification is not permitted, as it creates room for unauthorised transactions, subsidy abuse, and violations of the programme's integrity,” the ministry said.

items.title: IJN cardiothoracic surgeon is first Malaysian elected to prestigious American association
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 29 — For more than three decades, Prof Datuk Seri Dr Jeffrey Jeswant Dillon has spent countless hours in operating theatres handling some of Malaysia's most complex cardiothoracic surgery cases, while balancing the demands of clinical practice, academia, research and mentorship. Earlier this month, the Senior Consultant Cardiothoracic Surgeon at Institut Jantung Negara (IJN) added another milestone to his distinguished career when he became the first Malaysian elected as a member of the prestigious American Association for Thoracic Surgery (AATS), placing Malaysia on the global cardiothoracic surgery map. For Dr Jeswant, the recognition represents more than a professional accolade, reflecting his unwavering commitment and dedication to advancing cardiothoracic surgery both in Malaysia and internationally.

items.title: Loke says independent task force to probe Chan Sow Lin LRT derailment; services to resume June 3
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 29 — The train that derailed at the Chan Sow Lin LRT station yesterday will be hoisted and removed today, Transport Minister Anthony Loke announced. Conducting a site inspection this morning, Loke told the press that a thorough investigation of the track condition will be carried out by an independent special task force and the Land Public Transport Agency (APAD). “This morning, I visited the site with the special task force and APAD to see for myself the condition of the track that caused the derailment yesterday.

items.title: Storm blows off roofs of nine houses and surau in Kijal, no injuries reported
items.summary: CHUKAI, May 29 — Families rushed to safety after strong winds and heavy rain damaged nine houses and a surau in Kampung Yak Yah, Ibok, Kijal near here yesterday evening. Kijal assemblyman Datuk Razali Idris said the storm struck at about 4.30 pm, with most of the houses suffering severe roof damage while two homes were damaged after being struck by fallen trees. He said they were shocked when a durian tree suddenly toppled and crashed onto the house.

items.title: Malaysian Media Council aims to clear confusion over its role as industry regulator
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 29 — The Malaysian Media Council (MMC), established in 2025, has emerged as a new mechanism to strengthen self-regulation within the country’s media industry amid growing concerns over ethics, media freedom and challenges in the digital era. MMC secretary Radzi Razak said the council was set up to provide a fairer, more professional and independent platform to manage media-related issues, particularly as cases involving the industry are increasingly shaped by social media pressure and enforcement action. Radzi said the council’s primary mandate under the Malaysian Media Council Act 2025 (Act 868) is to develop a self-regulatory system for the media industry rather than function as a government enforcement agency.

items.title: Malaysia’s No. 1 Islamic economy title an achievement regardless of race or religion, says minister Nga Kor Ming
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, May 29 — Malaysia has retained its position as the world’s leading Islamic economy in 2025, a recognition that highlights the country’s strong Islamic financial system, halal industry, innovation and development ecosystem. Housing and Local Government Minister Nga Kor Ming said the achievement, based on the State of the Global Islamic Economy (SGIE) Report 2024/2025, reflected international confidence in Malaysia’s political stability, clear economic policies and overall development direction. He said the ranking demonstrated that the Madani Government’s emphasis on good governance, investor confidence and inclusive development continued to receive recognition at the global level.

items.title: ‘Mysterious’ one-day Johor assembly sitting on June 22 sparks fresh dissolution rumour
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, May 29 — The sudden calling of a "mysterious" one-day special sitting of the Johor State Legislative Assembly on June 22 has reignited intense speculation over the potential dissolution of the state government. Johor Jaya assemblyman Liow Cai Tung disclosed today that she had received a formal notice for the session, noting several "unprecedented" anomalies in the scheduling. “If it's not to dissolve the Johor State Legislative Assembly, then what is the agenda for this session?

items.title: Motorcyclist, 62, killed after MPV rear-ends bike on Sepupok Bridge in Miri
items.summary: Miri police chief ACP Mohd Farhan Lee Abdullah said initial police investigations found that the 62-year-old rider was travelling from Niah towards Miri when he was hit from behind by the MPV. Mohd Farhan said the case is being investigated under Section 41(1) of the Road Transport Act 1987 for causing death by dangerous driving. Mohd Farhan also advised road users to always practise prudent driving and comply with traffic regulations to avoid endangering other road users.

items.title: RM2,000 tickets and tough choices: Soaring airfares cast shadow over Gawai reunions for outstation Sarawakians
items.summary: For working adults such as Rossey La, 25, Sennia Rinna, 27, and Marieo Jimbai, 33, returning home remains a cherished tradition despite expensive festive airfares. Rossey, a cashier in Johor Bahru, said she still prioritises celebrating Gawai with her family even though ticket prices can be a financial strain. “However, I cannot deny that airfare prices significantly affect our decision to travel back home during Gawai,” she said.

items.title: Border chaos as ageing immigration system crashes again, stranding thousands around Malaysia
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 29 — Tens of thousands of travellers braved massive queues at all entry points across Malaysia yesterday, after the national immigration system collapsed once again following a major technical glitch. The system was not hacked. “The MyIMMs system is already 30 years old.

items.title: MetMalaysia forecasts heavy rainfall in Langkawi, Pontian, Kulai, JB
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 29 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) issued a thunderstorm warning for parts of Kedah and Johor, cautioning residents to brace for severe weather conditions this morning. The warning, issued at 8:55 am today, covers Langkawi in Kedah and the districts of Pontian, Kulai and Johor Bahru in Johor. Residents in the affected areas are advised to remain indoors where possible, avoid open spaces and stay away from flood-prone areas until conditions improve.

items.title: The great migration: Why Singapore giants like Gardenia are moving to Johor
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, May 29 — In Senai’s sprawling industrial belt, Gardenia Malaysia’s massive bakery sits alongside warehouses operated by tech giant Seagate and e-commerce heavyweight Shopee. Last week, the bread maker announced it would shift production from its Pandan Loop facility in Singapore to Johor Bahru, a move resulting in 141 retrenchments in the republic. “Moving operations to Johor makes strong commercial sense,” said Tan, CEO of Olive Tree Property Consultants.

items.title: Pahang Sultan extends condolences, aid to surviving children of couple killed in Pekan crash
items.summary: The Pahang Sultanate, in a Facebook post, said the contribution from His Royal Highness was presented by the Sultan of Pahang’s special private secretary, Tok Aria Rakna Ahmad Norbadelin Abdul Murad, to the family of Inspector Syah Lokman Ludza Ramli, 50, and his wife Nurulfiza Ardiana Ambak, 46, at the Tengku Ampuan Afzan Hospital (HTAA) here yesterday. According to the post, two of the couple’s children, Mohammad Adib Syah, 18, and Mohammad Aqif Syah, 10, are currently receiving treatment at the hospital for injuries sustained in the incident. Syah Lokman Ludza, who was attached to the Kuantan District Police Headquarters, and Nurulfiza Ardiana were reported to have died at the scene due to severe injuries.

items.title: Melaka police: Girl, 14, injures herself after adoptive mother bars meeting with boyfriend
items.summary: MELAKA, May 29 — A 14-year-old girl, believed to be under the influence of ‘mushroom’ vape, slashed her neck and wrists after being stopped by her adoptive mother from meeting her 17-year-old boyfriend at a house in Jonker Walk, Banda Hilir here. Melaka police chief Datuk Dzulkhairi Mukhtar said that in the 10.33 pm incident on Sunday (May 24), the victim, upon being prevented from meeting her boyfriend by her 49-year-old adoptive mother, went into a bedroom on the first floor of the house, before injuring herself with a knife. “The victim was taken to the Melaka Hospital and her condition is reported to be stable,” he said in a statement yesterday.

items.title: Man, 23, missing at sea off Langkawi after waves sweep him away while retrieving jetski
items.summary: ALOR SETAR, May 29 — A man is feared to have drowned after he tried to retrieve a jetski that drifted off in the waters near Langkawi today. Langkawi Fire and Rescue Department Zone 4 chief Fire Supt Mohd Zamri Abdul Ghani said they received a call about the incident at 9 pm and a team of five firefighters from the Persiaran Putra Fire and Rescue Station arrived at the location about 22 minutes later. “The water rescue team will begin search and rescue operations tomorrow morning to locate the victim,” he added.

items.title: APAD probes LRT derailment near Chan Sow Lin, demands full report from Rapid KL
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 29 — The Land Public Transport Agency (APAD) has launched a thorough investigation into the Light Rail Transit (LRT) train derailment near the Chan Sow Lin station on Thursday morning, warning of strict enforcement action if negligence is found. APAD said it conducted a site inspection immediately after the incident, led by its director-general, alongside representatives from the Rail Enforcement and Rail Planning divisions, to assess the situation and ensure immediate remedial actions were taken by the operator. Should any violations be found, enforcement action may be taken under Section 101 of the Land Public Transport Act 2010.

items.title: From ‘bah’ to ‘kamek’: The many differences between Sabahans and Sarawakians (VIDEO)
items.summary: But for many in peninsular Malaysia, Sabah and Sarawak are still casually bundled together as “East Malaysia” — or worse, reduced to the catch-all category of “others” on forms and official documents. “Malayans often see Sabah and Sarawak as one cultural block called ‘East Malaysia’, but to Sabahans and Sarawakians, the differences are obvious,” said Universiti Malaysia Sabah linguistics professor Jane Wong. “They share Borneo, but they do not share one single culture, one single language, or one single identity,” she said.

items.title: Putrajaya demands investigation into Malaysians detained by Israel in Gaza flotilla mission, insists rights and dignity be protected
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 28 — The Madani Government remains firm and will not compromise on protecting the safety, welfare and rights of Malaysians abroad, in line with international legal principles and universal humanitarian values. Minister of Home Affairs Datuk Seri Saifuddin Nasution Ismail said the government demands an immediate, comprehensive, independent and transparent investigation into the harsh treatment of Malaysian citizens and Global Sumud Flotilla (GSF) 2.0 activists detained by Israeli forces during a recent humanitarian mission to Gaza. “The government also demands that the welfare, dignity and legal rights of all detained activists be fully respected and protected,” he said in a post on his Facebook page today.

items.title: Putrajaya and Selangor must act fast to ‘lock in’ proposed PJ public hospital, says Kampung Tunku rep
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 28 — Kampung Tunku assemblyman Lim Yi Wei today urged Putrajaya and the Selangor government to move quickly on plans for a new public hospital in Petaling Jaya, warning that delays in land matters and funding could derail the project. Welcoming the recent announcement of a finalised site for the proposed hospital, Lim said the land should be immediately rezoned as a hospital site under the “Institutional and Public Facilities Zone”. Lim also warned that building a new hospital alone would not resolve deeper structural problems in the healthcare system.

items.title: One wild ‘Odyssey’: PJ police nab four suspected thieves after car chase from Kota Damansara to Air Panas
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 28 — Four local men were arrested after driving dangerously and fleeing from a police patrol car from Kota Damansara to Air Panas here early this morning. Petaling Jaya district police chief ACP Shamsudin Mamat said the incident began at 2.23am when members of a police patrol car tried to inspect a black Honda Odyssey multi-purpose vehicle (MPV). “The police’s swift action, however, succeeded in intercepting the vehicle at Jalan Air Jernih, Air Panas, Kuala Lumpur before arresting the four suspects,” he said in a statement today.
